steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank follows a long introductory prepositional phrase ('With a blend of traditional design elements... floor-to-ceiling windows') and precedes the main clause ('transitional design splits the difference between old and new'). The correct punctuation must properly connect an introductory phrase to a main clause.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — semicolon ('windows;'): A semicolon requires an independent clause on both sides. The introductory phrase has no verb and is not an independent clause. ✗
  • Choice B — period ('windows.'): A period would leave the introductory phrase as a sentence fragment with no verb. ✗
  • Choice C — em dash ('windows—'): An em dash signals a sharp break or parenthetical aside, not an introductory phrase leading into a main clause. ✗
  • Choice D — comma ('windows,'): A comma correctly separates the long introductory prepositional phrase from the main clause that follows. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
